codeReview.summary: The commit adds useful assertion-tagged tests, but two core assertions are only partially covered: VAL-EXEC-001 validates only the completed phase, and VAL-EXEC-012 does not verify run-journal event ordering or replay artifacts. As written, the diff does not fully substantiate deterministic phase mapping and replayable run-journal timeline behavior required by the feature contract.

issues.description: `testVAL_EXEC_001_RunPhaseToPacketStatusMappingIsDeterministic` only exercises `.completed` and explicitly documents that `.failed`/`.cancelled` are not covered, so deterministic phase→status mapping is not validated across terminal outcomes required by VAL-EXEC-001.

`testVAL_EXEC_012_RunJournalCapturesDeterministicReplayableExecutionTimeline` does not inspect run-journal events, ordering, or replay output; it only asserts final packet/result state (lines 2304-2306). This does not satisfy VAL-EXEC-012 evidence requirements for journal sequence + replay consistency.

Feature metadata includes VAL-EXEC-003, but this commit adds no assertion-tagged VAL-EXEC-003 case. Provenance checks remain in an untagged legacy test (`testMissionDispatchLinksRealRunAndSyncsResultProvenance`), reducing assertion traceability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `runtime-orchestration-worker` procedure asks workers to run `BurnBarProviderRouterTests` as part of focused validation, but this run omitted it while still marking `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `.factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md` step 5 includes `swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarDaemon --filter OpenBurnBarProviderRouterTests`; handoff verification commands list only MissionControl tests, full daemon tests, and extension controller unit tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered a repeatable harness limitation around failed/cancelled terminal-phase sync coverage, but this constraint is not captured in shared library notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `discoveredIssues` notes VAL-EXEC-001 was simplified because failed/cancelled terminal phases did not sync correctly in harness-only testing; `.factory/library/mission-control-m6-known-behaviors.md` does not document this testing caveat.

summary: Review failed. The diff adds new tests but does not fully validate two critical contract assertions: deterministic phase mapping across terminal phases (VAL-EXEC-001) and replayable run-journal timeline evidence (VAL-EXEC-012).
